methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $7.5B - $78.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Scale AI, a private Defense & Security software company founded by Alexandr Wang and Lucy Guo, commands a current valuation of $29B following $1.6B+ in total fundraising. The company operates within a competitive peer set of 10 comparable companies—2 public and 8 private—spanning similar maturity and sector positioning, including Anduril Industries (SPAC, $78.0B), Saronic (Series D, $9.2B), and Flock Safety (Series E, $7.5B).

Peer-median valuation analysis across three same-sector comparables with similar valuation tier positioning yields a median implied value of $9.2B, with a range spanning $7.5B (Flock Safety floor) to $78.0B (Anduril ceiling). This method serves as the primary triangulation point given data constraints. The wide dispersion reflects stage and commercial-stage heterogeneity within the Defense & Security cohort—Anduril's SPAC transition and scale differential create an outlier condition that limits comparability strictness.

Triangulation supports a valuation range of $7.9B (low) to $10.6B (high), anchored at $9.2B median. Scale AI's current $29B valuation substantially exceeds peer-derived estimates by a 3.2x multiple relative to median. Key uncertainties include proprietary revenue run-rate data, contract concentration with defense customers, government classification restrictions, and path-to-liquidity timing. Single-method reliance (peer-median only) constrains analytical robustness; revenue and EBITDA multiples would materially refine the estimate if available.
